mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with stable core inflation indicate expanding real purchasing power without triggering rate hikes, which fuels equity risk appetite and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and flat CPIAUCSL fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production contraction, a subsequent rebound in manufacturing output signals a supply-side recovery that lifts corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive following two-month INDPRO declines, or the signal yields a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust aggregate demand, which directly supports earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following consecutive PAYEMS increases are not statistically different from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.25 percent, the Federal Reserve signals a normalization phase that increases the cost of capital, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and industrial production indicates synchronized expansion across durable goods and construction sectors, which amplifies corporate order books and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The co-movement of rising HOUST and INDPRO fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a disinflationary environment where core prices fall, real GDP growth accelerates purchasing power without triggering monetary tightening, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL falls consecutively while GDPC1 expands.
